description.content:
The Exchange API is used by the Exchange admin center (`admin.exchange.microsoft.com`)
for same-origin shell bootstrap, user preference storage, mailbox and recipient detail
blades, role administration, mobile device management, organization sharing, mail flow
administration, migration state, public folder inventory, and report widgets.

The operations below were confirmed while browsing the Exchange admin center with a real
authenticated Edge session. They were observed on routes including:

  - Home/dashboard
  - Accepted domains
  - Admin roles
  - Alert policies
  - Connectors
  - Contacts
  - Groups
  - High Volume Email
  - Mobile device access
  - Mobile device mailbox policy
  - Mail flow reports
  - Migration
  - Organization sharing
  - Public folders
  - Resources
  - Remote domains
  - Settings
  - Troubleshoot
  - User roles

Requests relied on the authenticated Exchange admin center browser session, same-origin
`fetch`/XHR traffic, and `x-requested-with: XMLHttpRequest`. The observed cookie set
included `.AspNetCore.Cookies` plus companion chunk cookies on `admin.exchange.microsoft.com`.

Shared `admin.microsoft.com` support, SharePoint, and feedback calls were also present in the
live session, but those were not modeled here because they are either generic Microsoft 365
admin shell dependencies or already documented elsewhere in the repository.

Documentation and specifications have been provided on a best-efforts basis and may not be
100% accurate.
